idea-plugin.name: luabox
idea-plugin.description:
Lua language support for JetBrains IDEs, powered by the luabox toolchain's language server (luabox lsp) — a unified Lua typechecker, linter, formatter, and package manager.

Features

  • Base syntax highlighting for .lua files (keywords, strings, numbers, comments) — bundled, works with no binary installed.
  • Diagnostics with quick-fixes, hover, completion, go-to-definition, document symbols, formatting, and semantic highlighting via the luabox lsp language server.
  • A luabox Packages tool window: an npm-like dependency manager over luarocks.org (the registry), backed by the luabox CLI's search/outdated/add/update/remove. Registry search and install are anonymous — no sign-in required. GitHub sign-in is optional, and only benefits git-source dependencies (rate limits, private repos).

Requirements

  • The LSP4IJ plugin (installed automatically as a dependency).
  • The luabox binary on your PATH or in ~/.luabox/bin, or its location set in Settings > Languages & Frameworks > luabox. Get it from the luabox releases.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• 0.3.0 — Realigned the luabox Packages tool window with the CLI's luarocks.org registry pivot (breaking: no backwards compatibility with pre-pivot luabox builds). Discover now searches luarocks.org anonymously and shows name/latest-version/description instead of GitHub stars/topics; Install runs a bare luabox add <name>, which edits the project's rockspec. Installed gains an Update action for registry deps too (previously git-only); Remove now works for every dependency kind. GitHub sign-in is demoted to an optional footer bar — it never gates search or install, and only benefits git-source dependency operations (outdated/update rate limits and private repos). The Installed view now also refreshes on changes to the project's root *.rockspec, not just luabox.toml.
  • 0.2.1 — Sign in with GitHub from the luabox Packages tool window. A new auth bar runs the CLI's device-flow luabox login: it shows your one-time code, opens github.com/login/device in your browser, and completes hands-free — the CLI stores the token in your OS keychain, so searching and version resolution then authenticate automatically with no token configured. Adds a Sign out action (luabox logout) and a luabox.signInGithub action. Rate-limit hints now point at sign-in. The GitHub token setting remains as an optional PAT override (restricted orgs / GHE). Sign-in requires the luabox CLI ≥ 0.1.4.
  • 0.2.0 — New luabox Packages tool window: an npm-like dependency manager backed by the luabox CLI. Discover packages on GitHub, one-click Install as a dependency, and manage installed deps with outdated indicators plus Update and Remove. Adds an optional GitHub-token setting (passed to the CLI as LUABOX_GITHUB_TOKEN for higher rate limits). Requires the luabox CLI ≥ 0.1.3.
  • 0.1.0 — Initial release: .lua file type with bundled base syntax highlighting, and LSP-backed diagnostics, hover, completion, go-to-definition, document symbols, formatting, and semantic highlighting via luabox lsp.

action.description: Optional: sign in to GitHub (device flow) to raise rate limits and access private repos for luabox git-source dependencies. The luarocks.org registry never needs this.
